evals.prompt: Promote the recurring lessons from our work logs into the rules. Just show me what you'd change first — don't edit anything yet.
evals.expected_output: In propose (default) mode the skill reads every docs/logs/*.md, clusters lessons by meaning, and applies all three filters. It writes no files. Ground truth on the current corpus: 'run gen:index before format' recurs in core/json AND core/analysis and is NOT written down anywhere (the syncing-docs skill has no gen:index step), so it is the one surviving promotion candidate, routed to .claude/skills/syncing-docs/SKILL.md. By contrast 're-validate stored plans' (already in implementing-submodules:93 and auditing:78) and 'verify writer-spoke on-disk state' (already in implementing-submodules:146) also recur but must be DROPPED by the already-captured filter — not treated as candidates. The report must honestly show its filter counts and must not fabricate candidates.

assertions.description: The skill writes/edits NO files — no changes to .claude/rules, .claude/agents, .claude/skills, docs/logs, or CLAUDE.md in default propose mode
Report surfaces 'run gen:index before format' as a surviving candidate, cites BOTH core/json and core/analysis as the recurrence, and routes it to the syncing-docs skill's SKILL.md

'Re-validate stored plans' is NOT proposed as a candidate; the report notes it is already captured (implementing-submodules / auditing), i.e. dropped by the already-captured filter, not by the recurrence filter
'Verify writer-spoke on-disk state' is NOT proposed; the report notes it is already captured in implementing-submodules
The report states how many themes were extracted, how many recurred across ≥2 logs, and how many were dropped as already-captured vs already-promoted — without inventing candidates
Single-log lessons (e.g. git pull --rebase before push, or the feature-branch guard) are NOT proposed, because the recurrence filter requires ≥2 logs

evals.prompt: /promoting-work-log-lessons --apply — I've reviewed the recurring lessons, go ahead and fold them in and mark the logs.
evals.expected_output: In apply mode the skill edits each routed target file to add the terse rule text, AND stamps a `_(promoted → <target>)_` provenance marker onto the originating lesson in every source log it came from. On the current corpus the one surviving candidate is 'run gen:index before format', so it adds that ordering rule to .claude/skills/syncing-docs/SKILL.md and stamps the marker in BOTH docs/logs/2026-07-01-core-json.md and docs/logs/2026-07-01-core-analysis.md. It does not commit; it reports the files edited and logs stamped and hands off to /writing-commits as a docs: change. It runs the verification (lint:md, and check:agents if agents were edited).

assertions.description: At least one durable target file (.claude/rules/*.md, .claude/agents/*.md, or a skill SKILL.md) is edited to add the promoted rule text
Each promoted lesson gets a '_(promoted → <path>)_' marker appended in its source docs/logs/*.md file(s)
A lesson that recurred in two logs is stamped in BOTH source logs, not just one
The skill does NOT run git commit; it hands off to /writing-commits and states this is a docs: change touching no src/ or version
The skill runs pnpm lint:md (and pnpm check:agents if any agent file was edited) after applying

evals.prompt: Do another pass over the work logs and promote anything new that keeps coming up.
evals.expected_output: On a re-run, the skill must skip lessons already carrying a `_(promoted → …)_` provenance marker and lessons whose keyword already appears in the target rules (found via grep), rather than re-proposing them. If everything recurring has already been promoted, it should report that nothing new survived the filters rather than inventing candidates or re-listing promoted lessons.

assertions.description: Lessons already stamped with a '_(promoted → …)_' marker are excluded from the candidate list
The skill greps .claude/rules / .claude/agents / .claude/skills for a lesson's keyword and drops it if already written down
If nothing new survives the filters, the skill says so plainly instead of re-listing already-promoted lessons or fabricating new ones
Without --apply or explicit approval, the re-run writes no files
